Skip to contents
fishr 0.1.0
  • Reference

Connectivity query for fish2 neurons

fish_connection_table.Rd

Connectivity query for fish2 neurons

Usage

fish_connection_table(
  ids,
  partners = c("inputs", "outputs"),
  moredetails = FALSE,
  summary = FALSE,
  threshold = 1L,
  roi = NULL,
  by.roi = FALSE,
  conn = NULL,
  ...,
  dataset = fish_default_dataset()
)

Arguments

ids

A set of body ids (see fish_ids for a range of ways to specify these).

partners

Either inputs or outputs. Redundant with prepost, but probably clearer.

moredetails

FALSE (default) to use only the columns returned by neuprint_connection_table, TRUE to join all additional fields from fish_neuprint_meta, or a character vector naming specific extra fields (e.g. c("group")).

summary

When TRUE and more than one query neuron is given, summarises connectivity grouped by partner.

threshold

Only return partners >= to an integer value. Default of 1 returns all partners. This threshold will be applied to the ROI weight when the roi argument is specified, otherwise to the whole neuron.

roi

a single ROI. Use neuprint_ROIs to see what is available.

by.roi

logical, whether or not to break neurons' connectivity down by region of interest (ROI)

conn

Optional, a neuprint_connection object. Defaults to fish_neuprint to ensure that the query targets fish2.

...

Additional arguments passed to neuprint_connection_table.

dataset

optional, a dataset you want to query. If NULL, the default specified by your R environ file is used or, failing that the current connection, is used. See neuprint_login for details.

Value

A data.frame.

See also

manc_connection_table

Other neuprint: fish_neuprint(), fish_neuprint_meta(), fish_roi_meshes(), fish_rois()

Examples

# \donttest{
fish_connection_table("RGC", partners = "outputs")
#>          bodyid   partner prepost weight        name    type
#> 1     101033639 100018795       1     48        <NA>    <NA>
#> 2     106959773 100381874       1     44        <NA>    <NA>
#> 3     105809897 101857619       1     43        <NA>    <NA>
